topic: A college student discovers their roommate has been reading their journal
raw_output:
[story 1]

The sound of the page turning was the first thing that snagged my attention. I was halfway through sketching out my anxieties about finals week in my battered leather-bound journal, a private space I’d meticulously kept hidden under my bed. When I glanced over, the corner of my diary was slightly askew, and the light from the desk lamp caught the edge of the page. My stomach plummeted. I slowly turned my head toward the shared room. Liam was sitting at his desk, ostensibly studying, but his gaze was fixed, unnervingly, on my open book. He didn't look up, but the way his jaw was tight, the slight, almost imperceptible tightening around his eyes—it was a clear signal. My hands started to tremble, and I felt a hot flush creep up my neck. I slammed the book shut, the sound echoing too loudly in the quiet room. "Hey," I managed, my voice coming out thin and reedy. Liam blinked slowly, a small, unreadable smile playing on his lips. "Just checking out your notes?" His tone was too casual, too knowing. I snatched my journal, shoving it under my pillow, feeling every muscle in my body tense up. I needed to get out. Now.

[story 2]

I walked into the room expecting the usual comfortable chaos of shared living, but the atmosphere felt thick, like wading through syrup. My eyes landed on Liam, who was hunched over his laptop. He wasn't looking at his screen; he was staring intently at my desk. My heart began to hammer against my ribs, a frantic, uneven rhythm. I could feel the heat radiating off my face, spreading down to my chest. Every instinct screamed at me to bolt, to pretend I hadn't noticed the subtle shift in his posture. He finally lifted his eyes, and for a split second, I saw something in them—a flicker of something deeply private, something I hadn't intended for anyone to see. When his eyes met mine, he didn't apologize or look away. He just held the look, a quiet acknowledgment hanging between us, heavy and suffocating. I mumbled something about needing water, my voice cracking slightly. I retreated to my side of the room, pulling the blanket up to my chin, trying to shrink into the space. The silence that followed felt like an accusation.

[story 3]

It was a slow, agonizing realization. I’d left my journal open on the nightstand, a vulnerability I hadn't accounted for. When I finally dared to look over, the sight of Liam leaning over, his long fingers delicately tracing a line of handwriting, made my breath hitch in my throat. A wave of intense embarrassment washed over me, so profound it made my limbs feel heavy and clumsy. I couldn't move. I just stood there, frozen, my gaze locked on the scene unfolding beside me. When he finally pulled back, a small, almost secretive smile touched his mouth, I felt a sudden, desperate urge to disappear. I quickly snatched the journal, my fingers clumsy with sudden panic, and tucked it deep into my backpack, zipping it shut with unnecessary force. I turned away sharply, walking toward the door without a word, the sound of my own hurried footsteps sounding deafening in the sudden quiet. I could feel my cheeks burning, a relentless, uncomfortable heat.
